chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-784 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of main case 06.02.2026 None present for the Petitioner Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Addl. A.G Sindh along with DSP Muhammad Raheem Chandio, SDPO Husri, SIP Deewar Ali Brohi SHO PS Jhok Sharif, District Sujawal, SIP Arsalan Latif SHO Pabban ----- Through this Petition, the Petitioner seeks recovery of her daughter Heama who is alleged to be in illegal and unlawful detention of Respondents No.8 to 10. Pursuant to notice, Respondents No.5, 6, 7 and the Sub-Divisional Police Officer Husri as well have filed their comments/responses, which are taken on record. Respondent No.7 SHO PS Jhok Sharif present in Court along with his comments has also filed a copy of FIR bearing Crime No.68 of 2025 at PS Jhok Sharif for offence u/s 365-B, 34 PPC r/w Section 3(i)3 Ante-Rape Act 2021 pertaining to the alleged abduction of the Petitioner’s daughter namely Heema as well as Order dated 12.11.2025. Perusal of comments, reveals that prior to filing present Petition, the Petitioner had lodged the aforesaid FIR on 23.10.2025. Thereafter, for quashment thereof, the alleged detainee Heama had filed C.P.No.D-5277 of 2025 (Mst. Khadija @ Hema and another versus Province of Sindh and others) before the principal Bench of this Court at Karachi seeking quashment on the ground that she after embracing Islam had contracted marriage with one Ghulam Hussain (Petitioner No.2 in said Petition); and, that she had not been abducted or kidnapped by anyone. In said Petition, vide Order dated 29.10.2025, while issuing notices to the Respondents the Court ordered for protection.

Khadija @ Hema and another versus Province of Sindh and others) before the principal Bench of this Court at Karachi seeking quashment on the ground that she after embracing Islam had contracted marriage with one Ghulam Hussain (Petitioner No.2 in said Petition); and, that she had not been abducted or kidnapped by anyone. In said Petition, vide Order dated 29.10.2025, while issuing notices to the Respondents the Court ordered for protection. On 12.11.2025, in view of the statement of Petitioner No.1 Mst Khadija @ Hema recorded by the I.O., the I.O. undertook to file report in “C’ Class before the concerned Magistrate within three days and on such undertaking of the I.O. based on the statement of the Petitioner No.1, the Petition was disposed of accordingly. The SHO PS Jhok submits that the I.O. of the case has submitted the Report u/s 173 Cr.P.C. before the learned Magistrate concerned. In the above circumstances, it is not a case of illegal detention as the Petitioner’s daughter has already contracted marriage with one Ghulam Hussain after embracing Islam. The purpose of filing the Petition thus stands achieved; accordingly, the Petition is disposed of. JUDGE Ali Haider
